bullets.details: Researchers are testing 'Otis,' a new 3D AI interface built with Three.js that aims to improve information retention by moving users from linear 2D chat logs to spatial, navigable environments, potentially boosting recall by up to 400%.
bullets.topic: Spatial AI Interfaces for Information Retention

bullets.details: Market analysis from Logan Bartlett highlights that current AI growth is driven by measurable enterprise business utility, differentiating it from the Dotcom bubble through stronger vertical SaaS resilience and specific problem-solving applications.
bullets.topic: AI Market Resilience vs. Dotcom Era

bullets.details: 'OpenYak' has launched as a private, local-first desktop AI agent that automates office workflows, analyzes local files, and manages multi-platform messaging (Slack, Discord, WhatsApp) without cloud dependency.
bullets.topic: Local-First AI Agent for Workflow Automation

bullets.details: Chinese humanoid robotics companies are scaling rapidly, with players like Agibot, Ubtech, and Unitree targeting mass production runs in the thousands, signaling a potential inflection point for the sector.

New research combines weight compression with KV-cache optimization techniques, enabling large Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models like DeepSeek to run on 80% less VRAM than standard BF16 implementations.

Meta and Arm are partnering to co-develop custom, AI-optimized data center CPUs designed to handle massive training and inference workloads more efficiently than legacy silicon, with plans to open-source the hardware designs.
bullets.topic: Meta and Arm Co-Developing AGI CPUs

bullets.details: 'Datahike' introduces an infrastructure-free database model that treats data as immutable, content-addressed values, enabling cross-team querying and joins across storage backends like S3 without requiring APIs or message brokers.
bullets.topic: Immutable Database Model for Data Collaboration

bullets.details: Viral rumors claiming that OpenAI canceled a massive memory order from Samsung and SK Hynix, subsequently crashing DRAM prices, have been debunked as false; the agreement was only a non-binding Letter of Intent.

digest_summary: This week in tech: Researchers are boosting recall with spatial AI interfaces like 'Otis,' while analysts argue the current AI market is more resilient than the Dotcom era due to enterprise utility. New developments include 'OpenYak,' a private, local-first workflow agent, and a breakthrough in MoE model compression that drastically cuts VRAM usage. Additionally, Meta and Arm are collaborating on open-source AI CPUs, Chinese humanoid robotics are entering mass production, and new immutable database models are simplifying data collaboration—all while rumors of an OpenAI memory order collapse have been debunked.
digest_title: Spatial Interfaces, Local AI Agents, and the Evolution of AI Infrastructure
